interactions.summary: Jiang reflects on causation and objectivity, distinguishes agents from puppets, sketches competing empires, treats Iran as the Eurasian pivot, and rejects nation-states as real actors.

speaker_notes.note: Focus refs are primarily Jiang speaking; embedded questions are Jiang reading or paraphrasing midterm/audience prompts unless separately noted.

claims.claim: Jiang says he is now a player rather than only an observer, which constrains his prediction accuracy and objectivity.
Politicians can be agents supported by secret societies while still having agency, free will, and the possibility of betraying patrons for a higher calling.
Jiang expects a multipolar order with an American Technate, Russian Third Rome, and Pax Judaica competing and cooperating for perhaps 10 to 20 years.
America can retreat into a continental fortress and use naval control to block rival trade, making Iran the pivot for China's Belt and Road access and Afro-Eurasian integration.
Wars require institutional alignment and eschatology; one man cannot simply control a country into war.
Nation-states are made-up concepts and collections of elite interests; many Asian and Middle Eastern elites are loyal to overseas wealth, family, and networks rather than local people.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang marks the danger of becoming part of the system he predicts: no longer observer, now player.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I'm no longer an observer. I'm a player.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is a rare self-limitation on his predictive authority.

signature_moments.moment: Agents are not puppets; at some point Caesar, Napoleon, Hitler, Stalin, Trump, or Putin may defect toward a higher calling.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This complicates the secret-society model with human rupture.

signature_moments.moment: The people do not matter in the nation-state abstraction; the people are resources exploited by elites.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the people are resourced to be exploited by the elite
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the harshest compression of his anti-nation-state model.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Projected American continental fortress centered on Greater North America.
Projected Russian imperial/religious pole in Jiang's multipolar frame.
Projected Israel-centered/transnational-capital order in the Middle East.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-09 Jiang frames the coming order as multipolar rather than a continued unipolar American moment.
